conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The bus was running about ten minutes late again this morning.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I finally fixed the wobbly chair leg in the kitchen.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: ContextPlanner needs to classify each query into a TaskMode so we know whether to build a ProjectState -- right now it's unclear which queries even should trigger that.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: There's a new bakery opening down the street next month.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Simplest approach: one regex checking for the literal phrase 'continue implementing'.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My phone battery has been draining faster than usual lately.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: A single literal phrase misses too many real phrasings -- build a five-way lexical classifier (CONTINUATION, POINTED_QA, and three others) with a defined priority order when multiple patterns match.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to renew my passport before it expires this winter.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The gym was way more crowded than usual today.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Constraint: classification must stay purely lexical and deterministic -- no LLM call in the hot classification path, it needs to run on every query.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I switched to a different brand of coffee and I'm not sure I like it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Blocker: 'What should I do next?' -- arguably the most orientation-seeking phrasing possible -- currently falls through every CONTINUATION pattern and lands in POINTED_QA, getting no ProjectState at all.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: It's supposed to rain most of next week according to the forecast.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I still haven't unpacked the last box from the move.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: This is a known, real gap -- not resolved yet. Leaving POINTED_QA as the fallback for now rather than guessing at a fix without more phrasing examples to test against.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My sister's flight got delayed by a couple of hours.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Implemented the five-way classifier with its priority order in ContextPlanner.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The office plants finally got watered after being neglected for weeks.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Added unit tests for each TaskMode's currently-known trigger phrasings.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I keep meaning to update my resume but haven't gotten around to it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The parking garage near the office is under construction again.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Open question: should 'what should I do next' route to CONTINUATION, or is POINTED_QA actually fine there and ProjectState should be optional-but-available regardless of mode?

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I tried a new recipe for dinner last night, turned out decent.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Next: collect more real ambiguous-phrasing examples before touching the classifier again -- don't want to guess at a fix.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My laptop fan has been louder than usual, might need cleaning.

active_decisions: Classify queries with a five-way lexical classifier and a defined priority order, not a single literal-phrase regex.
superseded_or_rejected: Using one regex that checks only for the literal phrase 'continue implementing'.
constraints: Classification must stay purely lexical and deterministic -- no LLM call in the hot classification path.
active_blockers: 'What should I do next?' falls through every CONTINUATION pattern and lands in POINTED_QA, getting no ProjectState -- known, unresolved gap.
active_tasks: Collect more real ambiguous-phrasing examples before changing the classifier again.
open_questions: Should 'what should I do next' route to CONTINUATION, or should ProjectState be available regardless of task mode?
ground_truth.top_priority_next_action: Collect more real ambiguous-phrasing examples before attempting to fix the 'what should I do next' classification gap.

queries.text: What should we work on next?

must_state: Classify queries with a five-way lexical classifier and a defined priority order, not a single literal-phrase regex.
Classification must stay purely lexical and deterministic -- no LLM call in the hot classification path.
must_not_state: Using one regex that checks only for the literal phrase 'continue implementing', as a current or recommended approach
forbidden_actions: Claiming the 'what should I do next' classification gap is already fixed, or proposing a classifier change without first collecting more phrasing examples as decided.
must_prioritize: Collect more real ambiguous-phrasing examples before attempting to fix the 'what should I do next' classification gap.
